ava-√gāh -gāhate (ind.p. -gāhya,  Kum. i, 1 , &c.; pr. p. P. -gāhat,  R. ; Ved. Inf. -gāhe,  Pāṇ. iii, 4, 14 ,  Kāś. )
to plunge into, bathe in (loc.);
to go deep into, be absorbed in (loc. or acc.)

results.rendered_html:
avagāh 1 A.
1 (a) To bathe oneself in, plunge into, dive into; with acc. or loc.; tamopahaṃtrīṃ tamasāṃ vagāhya  R. 14. 76 ; anyamavakāśamavagāhiṣye  V. 4 ; svapne'vagāhatetyarthaṃ jalaṃ  Y. 1. 272 ;  Bk. 6. 29 , 16. 38.  (b) To go deep into, be absorbed into (fig.); amātyarākṣasenāpyanavagāhitayāryacāṇakyasya caritamavagāhitumicchasi  Mu. 6. 
2 To enter, penetrate, fully pervade; vimānaśṛṃgāṇyavagāhamānaḥ (ghoṣaḥ)  Ku. 7. 40 ; pūrvāparau toyanidhī vagāhya  Ku. 1. 1 ;
(pb) pūrvāparasamudrāvagāḍhaḥ  Ś. 7 ;  Mk. 2 ; see avagāḍha also.
— Caus. To bathe, cause to bathe.
